cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of Destiny: Embracing and Rejecting the Apocalypse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hellboy transitions from resisting his prophesied role as the harbinger of the apocalypse to momentarily embracing it, activating the ritual's first stages, before ultimately rejecting his demonic destiny by breaking off his horns and halting the ritual.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hellboy is symbolically depicted as the destroyer in an apocalyptic vision, foreshadowing his potential surrender to fate.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Hellboy actively defies his mythic identity, destroys the ritual's momentum, and reasserts his humanity by killing Grigory and severing his demonic connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Grigory's final words imply that Hellboy may not fully understand the nature of his power, leaving lingering doubt about whether his rejection of destiny is permanent.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Bonds: The Emotional Journey with Liz
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hellboy's relationship with Liz evolves through rejection, insecurity, reconciliation, and ultimate tragedy, culminating in her apparent death and his solitary resolve to protect her even in loss.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Liz rejects Hellboy emotionally and physically, severing their connection and leaving him heartbroken.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite her death, Hellboy remains committed to protecting Liz, entering a final confrontation with Behemoth driven by love and grief.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Liz's fate remains uncertain after her death, and Hellboy's emotional dependence on her continues to shape his actions beyond her apparent end.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Outcast to Reluctant Savior: The Crisis of Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hellboy grapples with his dual existence as a mythologized figure, a son, and a weapon of destiny, confronting paternal loss, existential manipulation, and public distortion before reclaiming agency over his identity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hellboy exists in a state of public myth and official denial, his true self obscured by media sensationalism.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After rejecting Grigory's claims and breaking his horns, Hellboy asserts self-determination, choosing humanity over prophecy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between how Hellboy sees himself and how others define him—father figures, institutions, the public—remains a persistent undercurrent.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Breaking the Chain: Defiance of Authority and Institutional Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Hellboy increasingly resists bureaucratic oversight and institutional authority, culminating in isolation and unilateral action that underscores his role as a rogue protector.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hellboy deactivates his locator and cuts ties with the BPRD, seeking autonomy after mission stress.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: After saving Manning only to be abandoned, Hellboy is physically and emotionally severed from command, operating entirely alone in hostile territory.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The rift between field autonomy and institutional control remains unhealed, threatening future cooperation and trust.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fatherless Son: Grief, Guilt, and Vengeance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Devastated by the death of Professor Broom, Hellboy channels his grief into personal vengeance, confronting Kroenen as a symbolic act of reclaiming justice for paternal loss.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hellboy arrives too late to see Broom alive, overwhelmed by guilt and the collapse of his emotional foundation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He achieves catharsis by defeating Kroenen, declaring him the killer of his father and asserting emotional dominance through violence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While Kroenen is defeated, the deeper void left by Broom's death persists, with no clear path to healing or replacement of that paternal bond.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Outsider to Keeper: Myers' Integration into the B.P.R.D.
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: John Myers transitions from a skeptical FBI transfer to an accepted agent within the B.P.R.D., beginning with his formal entry and culminating in his emotional confrontation with Hellboy, marking the start of his transformation into a committed member of the supernatural team.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Myers arrives as an outsider, physically uncomfortable and uncertain of his reception.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Myers accepts the surreal reality of the B.P.R.D. and begins forming a personal bond with Hellboy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Myers still grapples with fear and disbelief, indicating incomplete internalization of his new role.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Guidance: Myers' Moral Responsibility for Hellboy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Myers evolves from a self-doubting agent to the moral anchor for Hellboy, entrusted by Broom to guide him toward humanity, culminating in his desperate attempts to stop Hellboy's transformation and ultimately triggering his redemption.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Myers expresses doubt about his worthiness to lead or influence Hellboy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Myers becomes the catalyst for Hellboy's salvation, physically and symbolically restoring his humanity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his success, Myers' influence remains reactive rather than proactive, raising questions about his long-term role in Hellboy's life.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Unrequited Connection: The Emotional Arc with Liz Sherman
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Myers develops a deep emotional bond with Liz, progressing from professional outreach to intimate connection, only to witness her revival and reunion with Hellboy, leaving him in a position of quiet, unacknowledged longing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Myers attempts to establish trust with Liz as a negotiator and ally.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Myers silently accepts his emotional distance from Liz as she reunites with Hellboy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Myers' feelings remain unspoken and unreciprocated, leaving his emotional future uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Observer to Catalyst: Myers' Role in the Final Confrontation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Myers evolves from a supportive field agent to a pivotal figure in the climax, shifting from combat participant to ritual victim and ultimately to the decisive actor who disrupts the apocalypse by aiding Hellboy's redemption.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Myers proves his value by engaging in combat and supporting Hellboy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Myers becomes the key to preventing the apocalypse through a courageous, symbolic act.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his critical role, Myers remains physically vulnerable and dependent on others in high-threat scenarios.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Leadership in Crisis: The Collapse and Reaffirmation of Team Cohesion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Myers repeatedly attempts to assert operational and emotional leadership, only to face resistance from Hellboy and systemic instability after Broom's death, culminating in his isolation during critical moments despite his growing commitment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Myers seeks to maintain team coordination and mission integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Myers is entrusted with critical responsibility but remains emotionally and operationally isolated.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The tension between individual autonomy and team unity persists, with no lasting resolution in command dynamics or interpersonal trust.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Power and the Fear of Destruction
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Liz's arc traces the traumatic origin and recurring struggle with her pyrokinetic powers, beginning with their uncontrolled eruption in childhood, culminating in the catastrophic explosion that kills her mother, and continuing through institutionalization and failed containment. Her powers remain a source of internal and external conflict, symbolizing both her trauma and her perceived danger to others.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Liz is a child overwhelmed by abuse and emotional pain, with no understanding or control over her emerging powers.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Liz is recognized as both powerful and vulnerable, with documented fear of losing control and no effective method to manage her abilities, reinforcing her status as a threat and a victim.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Liz continues to fear that she will lose control and cause destruction, haunted by guilt and the unresolved trauma of her past.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Tentative Reconnection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc follows Liz's emotional journey from enforced isolation and psychological suppression to a fragile re-engagement with the B.P.R.D. and others. Triggered by Hellboy's presence and Myers' outreach, Liz gradually shifts from resistance to cautious reintegration, marked by small emotional breakthroughs despite her guardedness.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Liz is emotionally detached, institutionalized, and actively suppressing her past identity and connections.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Liz begins to re-engage with others, showing vulnerability and moments of joy, signaling a tentative return to social and emotional connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Liz's sense of entrapment and lack of true autonomy persists, raising questions about whether the B.P.R.D. offers healing or another form of confinement.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Lingering Bond with Hellboy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc explores the complex, unresolved emotional connection between Liz and Hellboy. Despite Liz's efforts to maintain distance and prioritize her healing, Hellboy's emotional dependence and their mutual history create persistent tension. Their relationship evolves from avoidance to a moment of mutual vulnerability, culminating in Liz's decision to stand by him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Hellboy is emotionally isolated, unable to process Liz's absence, while Liz actively resists reconnection to protect her autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Liz chooses to stay with Hellboy, affirming their bond despite his self-doubt and monstrous identity, marking a shift toward shared emotional intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fundamental incompatibilities in their identities and roles remain, leaving uncertain whether their connection can endure long-term.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reclaiming Agency Amid Institutional Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc charts Liz's shifting status from a confined, passive patient to an active agent making choices about her future. Initially stripped of autonomy in maximum security, she gradually regains agency through Myers' offer, emotional re-engagement, and ultimately her decision to join the Moscow mission, reclaiming her role and identity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Liz is fully isolated in a maximum-security padded cell, perceived as a threat and stripped of autonomy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Liz actively chooses to commit to a dangerous mission, asserting her agency and redefining her place within the B.P.R.D. on her own terms.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The extent to which the B.P.R.D. truly respects Liz's autonomy remains questionable, as institutional and emotional pressures continue to shape her choices.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Shadow of Prophecy and Psychic Manipulation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc centers on the external forces seeking to manipulate Liz's powers for apocalyptic ends. Grigory's psychic reactivation of her connection to Rasputin's plan threatens to override her hard-won control, linking her past trauma to a looming supernatural threat. Her powers are not just personal but part of a larger, dangerous design.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Liz is vulnerable to psychic intrusion, with Grigory triggering a subconscious response tied to Rasputin's prophecy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Liz is in active danger, trapped in a cavern with advancing Sammael creatures, her earlier reactivation possibly linked to the unfolding threat.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full extent of the psychic trigger's effects and the identity of the 'child' in the prophecy remain unknown, leaving Liz's role in the larger conflict ambiguous.
